_meta.description: Bundled baseline of common high-pressure (DAC) reference phases: pressure markers, gasket materials, and pressure media. Each entry carries a 3rd-order Birch-Murnaghan EOS (V0 in Angstrom^3, K0 in GPa, K0p dimensionless) and, where the structure is simple, the space group + ambient lattice + asymmetric-unit atoms so peak positions and relative intensities can be simulated.
_meta.disclaimer: These are reference STARTING values compiled from common high-pressure literature at ~300 K. Reported EOS parameters vary between studies (and are correlated, especially K0 vs K0'). Verify K0/K0' against the standard and analysis protocol you actually use before quantitative pressure determination. User entries in the workspace shadow these by name.

phases.name: Au
phases.source: Anderson, Isaak & Yamamoto (1989), J. Appl. Phys. 65, 1534.
phases.notes: Classic FCC pressure marker; many alternative scales exist (e.g. Tsuchiya 2003, Fei 2007, Dorogokupets & Dewaele 2007).

phases.name: Pt
phases.source: Holmes, Gathers & Bassett (1989), J. Appl. Phys. 66, 2962.
phases.notes: FCC. Stiff and chemically inert; good high-pressure marker. See also Dorogokupets & Dewaele (2007).

phases.name: Cu
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.

phases.name: Ag
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.

phases.name: W
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.
phases.notes: BCC. Also frequently used as a gasket material.

phases.name: Mo
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.

phases.name: MgO
phases.source: Speziale, Zha, Duffy, Hemley & Mao (2001), J. Geophys. Res. 106, 515.
phases.notes: Periclase, rocksalt (B1) structure. Common internal standard, stable to very high P.

phases.name: NaCl-B1
phases.notes: Rocksalt (B1) phase. Transforms to the CsCl-type B2 phase near ~30 GPa (room T) — this entry is only valid below the transition. Also widely used as a soft pressure medium.

phases.name: Re
phases.source: Anzellini et al. (2014); Dubrovinsky et al.
phases.notes: HCP. The most common DAC gasket; its rings nearly always appear in the pattern, so it is useful both as a contaminant to recognize and as a secondary marker.

phases.name: Ne
phases.source: Dewaele, Datchi, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2008), Phys. Rev. B 77, 094106.
phases.notes: Solid Ne (FCC) above ~4.8 GPa. Excellent quasi-hydrostatic medium; V0/K0 are EOS-fit extrapolations to zero pressure, not the ambient solid.

phases.name: Ar
phases.source: Ross, Mao, Bell & Xu (1986); Dewaele et al. (2008).
phases.notes: Solid Ar (FCC). Quasi-hydrostatic medium; EOS parameters are zero-pressure extrapolations.

phases.name: He
phases.source: Loubeyre et al. (1993), Phys. Rev. Lett. 71, 2272.
phases.notes: Best hydrostatic medium available, but extremely compressible and a weak X-ray scatterer (Z=2) — its Bragg signal is usually negligible. Structure/EOS intentionally left blank here; fill in from Loubeyre (1993) if you need to model it.

phases.name: Stainless steel (gasket)
phases.notes: Common gasket alloy (e.g. 301/304). Polycrystalline BCC/FCC mixture with composition-dependent lattice; no single clean EOS is bundled. Listed so its rings can be flagged as a known contaminant; supply a CIF/EOS if you need quantitative treatment.
